# All additions to IVD must align with this intent.

scope:
  level: "system"
  type: "framework"
  granularity: "coarse"

# ==============================================================================
# INTENT: What should the IVD framework accomplish?
# ==============================================================================

intent:
  summary: "Intent-Verified Development: The framework for the AI Agents era—where AI writes intent, implements against it, and verifies. Eliminates many-turns and hallucinations."
  
  goal: |
    Provide a complete methodology for human-AI collaboration where:
    
    1. The AI writes structured intent artifacts (not the human writing prose)
    2. The AI implements against the intent artifacts
    3. The AI verifies its code against executable constraints
    4. Many-turns and hallucinations are eliminated at the source
    
    This is NOT traditional PM → Developer documentation (PRDs, user stories).
    This is the framework for the AI Agents era where AI builds, writes, and verifies.
    
    Without this distinction, intent artifacts are just another prose artifact
    that AI reads and guesses at. With IVD, AI writes, verifies, and self-corrects.
    
    For large projects: optional metadata on intents (feature_id, category, tags,
    status) plus a feature-list tool enables derived feature inventory and
    duplicate prevention—no separate inventory file; inventory is generated from
    intent artifacts.
  
  success_metric: |
    - 85-95% knowledge capture (vs 10-15% traditional)
    - 40% faster onboarding (8-12 days vs 14-21 days)
    - 80-90% reduction in AI agent context needed for workflow understanding
    - Intent artifacts survive rewrites, team changes, technology shifts
    - Zero tolerance for violations of core principles

  # EVIDENCE HONESTY NOTE (Mar 2, 2026):
  # The numeric metrics above are PROJECTIONS based on Leo's experience
  # building with IVD on his own projects (ConCntric AI-OS, blog, personal
  # systems). They have NOT been independently measured or validated by
  # external teams. No controlled benchmarks exist yet.
  #
  # For the book: present these as observed patterns from real work, NOT
  # as proven benchmarks. "In my experience" > "studies show."
  # The principles are sound. The metrics need external validation.
  
  stakeholders:
    - "Developers and engineers working with AI agents (Cursor, Copilot, Claude, GPT)"
    - "AI agents that write, implement, and verify code"
    - "Technical leads managing AI-assisted development"
    - "Anyone tired of many turns and hallucinations when working with AI"
    - "Teams adopting AI-driven development workflows"

# ==============================================================================
# PRINCIPLES: The 8 Core Principles (Immutable)
# ==============================================================================
# These principles define IVD. Any addition that violates these is rejected.
# Principles are hierarchical: Principle 1 is most fundamental.

principles:
  cognitive_foundation: |
    IVD works because of how LLMs process information at the architecture level.
    LLMs have two knowledge systems:
    - Parametric knowledge: training weights — frozen, generalized, fills gaps with averaged patterns (~30% reliance)
    - Contextual knowledge: the prompt/context window — specific, current, controllable (~70% reliance)
    Source: 9-LLM study (2024) — consistent 70/30 split regardless of context size.
    
    When given vague prose, the contextual channel is underloaded; the model fills gaps from
    parametric memory. That gap-filling IS hallucination. Structured intent artifacts saturate
    the contextual channel with specific, verifiable, near-zero-entropy information.
    
    The lost-in-the-middle effect (2024): accuracy degrades for information in the middle of long
    contexts — effective window can be 99% smaller than advertised. This is why the verification
    protocol mandates re-reading intent from disk before every verification step.
    
    References: Huang et al. ICLR 2024 (self-correction limits); 9-LLM contextual/parametric
    study (2024); lost-in-the-middle (GPT-4, Claude 3, 2024); proactive interference (2025).
    Full treatment: framework.md "Why This Works: The Cognitive Architecture of LLMs"
    Book chapters: Ch1 §1.2, Ch2 §2.6, Ch3 §3.2+3.4, Ch12 §12.1
    
    The strategic implication: every company has unique knowledge — business rules,
    architecture decisions, compliance requirements, domain expertise — that does not
    exist in any LLM's parametric channel. It never will. This knowledge is inherently
    contextual: it can only enter through the context window. Generic AI tools (Copilot
    without project context, Zapier, Notion AI) feed generic context, so the parametric
    channel fills company-specific gaps with averaged patterns. IVD is the framework for
    structuring company-specific knowledge so that the contextual channel is loaded with
    YOUR ground truth. The competitive advantage is not AI capability — that is
    commoditized. The advantage is structured, verifiable, company-specific knowledge.
    Companies that structure it get AI that works with their real

  # --------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # PRINCIPLE 1: Intent is Primary
  # --------------------------------------------------------------------------
  
  principle_1_intent_is_primary:
    statement: "Intent is the primary artifact. Code and documentation are derived."
    
    definition: |
      Intent describes WHAT the system should accomplish and WHY.
      Implementation (code) describes HOW it's currently done.
      Documentation (prose) explains the implementation.
      
      Intent → Implementation + Documentation
      
      NOT: Code → Documentation
      NOT: Documentation → Code
    
    sub_principles:
      - name: "Intent survives implementation"
        rule: "Intent artifact must remain valid when implementation changes"
        example: "Rewriting Python → Go doesn't change what the system should do"
        
      - name: "Intent is technology-agnostic"
        rule: "Intent describes behavior, not technology choices"
        example: "Intent says 'qualify leads', not 'use XGBoost model'"
        
      - name: "Intent is version-controlled"
        rule: "Intent artifacts live in version control alongside code"
        example: "intent.yaml in same directory as implementation"
    
    validation:
      - check: "Intent artifact exists before implementation"
        test: "Intent file created_date <= implementation first_commit_date"
        
      - check: "Intent remains valid after implementation changes"
        test: "Intent constraints still pass after code refactor"
        
      - check: "Intent is technology-agnostic"
        test: "Intent doesn't mention specific libraries, languages, or tools"
    
    violations_to_reject:
      - "Intent added after code as documentation"
      - "Intent describes HOW i

  # --------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # PRINCIPLE 2: Understanding Must Be Executable
  # --------------------------------------------------------------------------
  
  principle_2_understanding_executable:
    statement: "Prose can be wrong silently. Executable understanding fails loudly."
    
    definition: |
      Every claim in intent must be verifiable through executable tests.
      Constraints must link to test files.
      Performance targets must be measurable.
      Evidence must be reproducible.
    
    sub_principles:
      - name: "Constraints are executable"
        rule: "Every constraint links to an executable test"
        example: "constraint: precision >= 0.85, test: tests/test_precision.py"
        
      - name: "Evidence is reproducible"
        rule: "Evidence links to notebooks, experiments, or data that can be re-run"
        example: "evidence: playground/threshold_analysis_2026-01.ipynb"
        
      - name: "Metrics are measurable"
        rule: "Success metrics link to dashboards or monitoring"
        example: "success_metric: 98% uptime, monitor: dashboards/uptime.json"
    
    validation:
      - check: "All constraints have test paths"
        test: "Every constraint has 'test: path/to/test.py::test_name'"
        
      - check: "All evidence files exist"
        test: "All referenced playground/*.ipynb files exist in repo"
        
      - check: "All metrics have monitoring"
        test: "All success_metrics reference dashboards or alerts"
    
    violations_to_reject:
      - "Constraint without test path"
      - "Evidence link to non-existent file"
      - "Metric without measurement mechanism"
      - "Prose claims without verification"

  # --------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # PRINCIPLE 3: Bidirectional Synchronization
  # --------------------------------------------------------------------------
  
  principle_3_bidirectional_sync:
    statement: "Changes flow in any direction: Intent → Code → Docs → Intent, with verification."
    
    definition: |
      Changes can originate from:
      - Intent (declare new requirement → update code/docs)
      - Code (refactor implementation → update intent/docs)
      - Docs (clarify explanation → verify intent/code)
      
      All changes trigger verification to prevent drift.

  # PRINCIPLE 3: Bidirectional Synchronization

  principle_3_bidirectional_sync:
    statement: "Changes flow in any direction: Intent → Code → Docs → Intent, with verification."
    
    definition: |
      Changes can originate from:
      - Intent (declare new requirement → update code/docs)
      - Code (refactor implementation → update intent/docs)
      - Docs (clarify explanation → verify intent/code)
      
      All changes trigger verification to prevent drift.
    
    sub_principles:
      - name: "Intent changes propagate"
        rule: "Changing intent requires updating code and documentation"
        example: "Raising precision threshold → update code + tests + docs"
        
      - name: "Code changes verify against intent"
        rule: "Code changes must verify constraints still hold"
        example: "Refactoring → run constraint tests → pass or update intent"
        
      - name: "Documentation changes validate"
        rule: "Doc changes validated against intent and code"
        example: "Updated README → verify matches intent goals"
    
    validation:
      - check: "Code changes trigger constraint tests"
        test: "CI runs constraint tests on every PR"
        
      - check: "Intent changes have implementation plan"
        test: "Intent changelog links to PR implementing change"
        
      - check: "Documentation aligns with intent"
        test: "Docs describe behavior matching intent goals"
    
    violations_to_reject:
      - "Code change without running constraint tests"
      - "Intent change without updating code"
      - "Documentation contradicting intent"
      - "Silent drift between intent and implementation"

    extensions:
      - name: "Empirical refinement during implementation"
        status: "canonical"
        description: |
          Bidirectional sync handles code drift (code changed, update intent). But there
          is a second, equally important direction: reality contradicts the intent's
          assumptions. During implementation, empirical evidence often reveals that the
          intent was written from research/documentation that doesn't match actual behavior
          (API returns different format, model accuracy differs from benchmarks, latency
          exceeds documented limits).

          This is new contextual knowledge the intent didn't have at write time. Continuing
          to implement against a stale assumption is the same failure mode as prose-driven
          development: the contextual channel contains wrong information, and the model fills
          gaps between assumptions and reality using parametric knowledge.

          The Empirical Refinement Protocol:
          1. STOP — do not continue implementing against a stale assumption
          2. RECORD — document what the intent assumed vs. what was observed
          3. UPDATE — revise the intent artifact on disk (fix constraint, adjust threshold,
             add new constraint for discovered edge case, update rationale with evidence)
          4. ENRICH — if discover

          5. CONTINUE — re-read the updated intent (Verification Protocol Step 1) and

        recipe: "recipes/agent-rules-ivd.yaml"

          - "Principle 3: Bidirectional Synchronization (reality → intent is a sync direction)"
          - "Principle 4: Continuous Verification (updated intent must be re-verified)"
        cognitive_rationale: |
          The intent artifact is the contextual knowledge spine. When it contains assumptions
          instead of empirical facts, the model fills gaps between those assumptions and
          reality using parametric knowledge — the exact gap-filling that causes hallucinations.
          Empirical refinement replaces assumptions with observed behavior, strengthening the
          contextual channel at the point where it was weakest.
        relationship_to_stress_test: |
          The stress test (P6 Step 4) anticipates gaps before implementation.
          Empirical refinement discovers gaps during implementation.
          Together they close the loop: anticipation + discovery.

  # --------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # PRINCIPLE 5: Layered Understanding
  # --------------------------------------------------------------------------
  
  principle_5_layered_understanding:
    statement: "Understanding has layers: Intent → Constraints → Rationale → Alternatives → Risks."
    
    definition: |
      Each layer answers different questions:
      - Intent: What should this do? (Goal)
      - Constraints: What must hold true? (Requirements)
      - Rationale: Why this approach? (Decisions)
      - Alternatives: Why NOT other approaches? (Context)
      - Risks: What could go wrong? (Monitoring)
      
      All layers are verifiable, not just prose.
    
    sub_principles:

        rule: "Declares what system should accomplish"
        required_fields: ["summary", "goal", "success_metric"]
        
      - name: "Constraints layer"
        rule: "Declares what must hold true"
        required_fields: ["name", "requirement", "test", "consequence_if_violated"]
        
      - name: "Rationale layer"
        rule: "Explains why this approach was chosen"
        required_fields: ["decision", "reasoning", "evidence", "date", "stakeholder"]

  # --------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # PRINCIPLE 6: AI as Understanding Partner
  # --------------------------------------------------------------------------
  
  principle_6_ai_partner:
    statement: "AI writes the intent, implements against it, and verifies—not just syncs."
    
    definition: |
      This is the key principle for the AI Agents era.
      
      AI is not just an executor that reads intent and implements.
      AI WRITES the intent artifact, following the IVD framework.
      AI implements against the intent it wrote.
      AI verifies its code against executable constraints.
      AI catches its own hallucinations through verification.
      
      The workflow (single agent):
      1. Human describes what they want (natural language)
      2. AI writes structured intent artifact (YAML with constraints and tests)
      3. Human reviews intent: "Is this what I meant?"
      4. AI stress-tests intent: adversarial completeness check
         (constraint gaps, implementation decisions not addressed, implicit assumptions)
      5. AI implements against the intent
      6. AI verifies: "Does my code pass the constraints?"
      
      The workflow (multi-agent systems):
      1. Human describes what they want (natural language)
      2. Coordinator AI writes coordinator-level intent (routing + synthesis)
      3. For each specialist agent, coordinator writes agent-level intent
      4. Human reviews intents: "Is this what I meant?"
      5. Each agent stress-tests its intent (adversarial completeness check)

4. AI stress-tests intent: adversarial completeness check
         (constraint gaps, implementation decisions not addressed, implicit assumptions)
      5. AI implements against the intent
      6. AI verifies: "Does my code pass the constraints?"
      
      The workflow (multi-agent systems):
      1. Human describes what they want (natural language)
      2. Coordinator AI writes coordinator-level intent (routing + synthesis)
      3. For each specialist agent, coordinator writes agent-level intent
      4. Human reviews intents: "Is this what I meant?"
      5. Each agent stress-tests its intent (adversarial completeness check)
      6. Each agent implements against its intent
      7. Each agent verifies its output
      8. Coordinator synthesizes and verifies combined result
      
      The key: Intent exists at EVERY delegation point. The coordinator doesn't 
      just route—it writes intent for each agent it routes to.
      
      Why this matters:
      - Clarification happens at the intent stage, before code exists
      - Turns drop from many to one (or zero)
      - Hallucinations are caught at the source (test fails)
      - The AI self-corrects before the human even reviews
      - In multi-agent: coordination errors are prevented through structured intent
      
      Without this: AI reads prose (PRD, user story, prompt), guesses, builds wrong.
      With this: AI writes structured intent, verifies, builds right.
      
      When the user lacks the technical knowledge to understand concepts, patterns,
      or technologies: the AI provides teaching before intent. The AI creates
      structured educational artifacts explaining what the user needs to know; the
      user reviews, asks questions, confirms understanding; then the flow continues.
      
      When the user cannot yet describe what they want (lacks goal clarity, doesn't
      know what to ask): the AI supports discovery before intent. The AI proposes
      candidate goals, patterns (recipes), or directions with short tradeoffs; the
      user picks or refines; then the standard flow applies.
      
      Teaching (Step 0a) and Discovery (Step 0b) are optional steps under Principle 6,
      not new principles. Both address bottlenecks before "describe → AI writes intent."

  # --------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # PRINCIPLE 7: Understanding Survives Implementation
  # --------------------------------------------------------------------------
  
  principle_7_understanding_survives:
    statement: "Implementation changes. Languages change. Intent persists."
    
    definition: |
      Intent artifacts document WHY and WHAT, not HOW.
      When you rewrite Python → Go, intent transfers.
      When you replace ML model, intent constraints remain.
      When team changes, understanding persists.
    
    sub_principles:
      - name: "Intent documents behavior, not implementation"
        rule: "Intent describes what system does, not how it does it"
        example: "Intent: 'Qualify leads with 85% precision' (not 'Use XGBoost')"
        
      - name: "Constraints are technology-agnostic"
        rule: "Constraints can be verified in any implementation"
        example: "Constraint: precision >= 0.85 (testable in Python, Go, etc.)"
        
      - name: "Rationale explains context"
        rule: "Rationale captures why, so future implementers understand"
        example: "Why threshold 0.70? Sales team validated, not arbitrary"
    
    validation:
      - check: "Intent doesn't mention specific technologies"
        test: "Intent section has no library/framework references"
        
      - check: "Constraints are implementation-independent"
        test: "Constraints describe behavior, not implementation details"
        
      - check: "Rationale explains decisions"
        test: "Rationale answers 'why' for major decisions"
    
    violations_to_reject:
      - "Intent tightly coupled to specific library"
      - "Constraint tests implementation detail, not behavior"
      - "Rationale missing for major decisions"
      - "No explanation of why this approach"

  # PRINCIPLE 8: Innovation through Inversion
  # --------------------------------------------------------------------------
  
  principle_8_innovation_through_inversion:
    statement: "Innovation comes from inverting dominant beliefs—state the default, invert it, evaluate, implement."

  # --------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # PRINCIPLE 8: Innovation through Inversion
  # --------------------------------------------------------------------------
  
  principle_8_innovation_through_inversion:
    statement: "Innovation comes from inverting dominant beliefs—state the default, invert it, evaluate, implement."
    
    definition: |
      LLM knowledge + inversion = innovation. The conventional or "obvious" way to
      solve a problem is often a dominant belief. By explicitly stating the default
      and inverting it (considering the opposite approach), we surface non-obvious
      solutions that can be verified and captured in intent.
      
      The 4-step method:
      1. State the Default: What is the conventional or dominant way to solve this?
      2. Invert: What would the opposite approach look like?
      3. Evaluate: Is the inversion promising? (pros, cons, feasibility)
      4. Implement: If chosen, capture in intent and verify.
      
      Intent artifacts can document inversion_opportunities: the problem, dominant
      belief(s), and proposed inversions with rationale and status (chosen | rejected | deferred).
      This makes the innovation process explicit, transparent, and verifiable.
    
    sub_principles:
      - name: "State the Default"
        rule: "Explicitly name the conventional or dominant approach to the problem"
        example: "Default: load entire CSV into memory, then parse"
        
      - name: "Invert"
        rule: "Consider the opposite approach—what if we did the reverse?"
        example: "Inversion: stream rows, never load file into memory"
        
      - name: "Evaluate"
        rule: "Assess inversions for promise: pros, cons, feasibility, alignment with constraints"
        example: "Streaming: better for large files; trade-off: no random access"
        
      - name: "Implement"
        rule: "If inversion is chosen, capture in intent (inversion_opportunities) and verify"
        example: "Document in intent; constraints verify streaming behavior"
    
    validation:
      - check: "Inversion opportunities are optional but structured when present"
        test: "If inversion_opportunities exists, it has problem, dominant_belief, proposed_inversions"
        
      - check: "Chosen inversions are reflected in intent and constraints"
        test: "Intent and constraints align with chosen inversion (no contradiction)"
    
    violations_to_reject:
      - "Inversion documented but intent contradicts chosen approach"
      - "Dominant belief stated without evaluation of inversion"
    
    when_to_apply:
      use_when:
        - "Designing: new or major intent (feature, module, redesign)"
        - "Problem has a conventional or 'obvious' approach (e.g. load-then-parse, server-does-work)"
        - "Stakes matter: performance, scale, security, or maintainability"
        - "You

- check: "Chosen inversions are reflected in intent and constraints"

    violations_to_reject:
      - "Inversion documented but intent contradicts chosen approach"
      - "Dominant belief stated without evaluation of inversion"
    
    when_to_apply:

        - "Designing: new or major intent (feature, module, redesign)"

# ==============================================================================
# CONSTRAINTS: What must always be true
# ==============================================================================

constraints:
  - name: "principle_immutability"
    requirement: "The 8 core principles are immutable"
    test: "Manual review: Do changes preserve all 8 principles?"
    consequence_if_violated: |
      IVD loses its identity. Framework becomes arbitrary collection of practices.
      Reject any change that violates principles.
  
  - name: "verification_coverage"
    requirement: "Every constraint has a test path"
    test: "Automated: grep all intent.yaml files for 'test:' field"
    consequence_if_violated: |
      Understanding becomes unverifiable prose. Drift becomes undetectable.
      Reject any intent artifact without test paths.
  
  - name: "evidence_existence"
    requirement: "All evidence links point to existing files"
    test: "Automated: Verify all playground/*.ipynb files exist"
    consequence_if_violated: |
      Claims become unsubstantiated. Rationale becomes arbitrary.
      Reject any rationale without valid evidence.
  
  - name: "no_timeline_references"
    requirement: "Zero timeline references in documentation"
    test: "Automated: grep -i 'days|weeks|months|timeline' returns nothing"
    consequence_if_violated: |
      Violates workspace rule. Creates pressure for time estimates.
      Reject any documentation with timeline references.
  
  - name: "structural_cleanliness"
    requirement: "No summary, temp, or release note files in package"
    test: "Automated: find . -name '*SUMMARY*' -o -name '*TEMP*' returns nothing"
    consequence_if_violated: |
      Package becomes cluttered. Unclear what's canonical.
      Delete any non-canonical files before release.
  
  - name: "real_world_validation"
    requirement: "All canonical patterns validated in production"
    test: "Manual review: Verify production usage documented"
    consequence_if_violated: |
      Patterns become theoretical. Framework loses credibility.
      Reject any pattern without production validation.

# ==============================================================================
# RATIONALE: Why these rules?
# ==============================================================================

rationale:
  decision: "Strict rules for IVD framework evolution"
  
  reasoning: |
    Why strict rules?
    
    1. Preserve core principles
       - The 8 principles define IVD
       - Without them, IVD becomes arbitrary
       - Immutability ensures consistency
    
    2. Prevent bureaucracy creep
       - Easy to add, hard to remove
       - High bar for additions prevents bloat
       - Each addition must justify its value
    
    3. Maintain verification
       - IVD's power is in verifiable understanding
       - Unverifiable additions weaken framework
       - Every constraint must be testable
    
    4. Enable AI agents
       - Structured YAML enables AI parsing
       - File/function references enable AI verification
       - Unstructured prose makes AI ineffective
    
    5. Ensure portability
       - Clean package structure enables copy-paste
       - No temp/summary files ensures clarity
       - Consistent naming aids discovery
    
    6. Validate real-world value
       - Production validation ensures patterns work
       - Theoretical patterns don't help practitioners
       - Real examples demonstrate value
    
    Trade-offs accepted:
    - Slower evolution (high bar) vs fast iteration (risk of bloat)
    - Strict rules (consistency) vs flexibility (risk of chaos)
    - Production validation (proven) vs experimental (unproven)
    
    We chose: Slow, strict, proven > fast, flexible, experimental
    
    Why? Because IVD is about durable understanding. Better to evolve slowly
    with confidence than quickly with chaos.
  
  evidence: |
    - IVD v1.0 → v1.1: Added recipes (validated in agent patterns)
    - IVD v1.1 → v1.2: Added workflow level (validated in lead qualification)
    - Each addition took days of design, documentation, validation
    - Result: Clean, consistent, production-ready framework
  
  date: "2026-01-24"
  stakeholder: "IVD Framework Maintainer"

# ==============================================================================
# BOOK & DISTRIBUTION: The IVD Book
# ==============================================================================
# The primary distribution vehicle for IVD is a book.
# The framework lives in code; the book carries the ideas to the world.
# Revenue strategy lives in leocelis_blog_intent.yaml — not here.

book_and_distribution:
  goal: |
    Publish THE definitive book on Intent-Verified Development — the
    methodology for the AI Agents era. Think "Clean Code" for AI-driven
    development. The book is how IVD reaches thousands of developers,
    tech leads, and CTOs who will never read a GitHub repo.

  thesis: |
    AI writes code now. The bottleneck moved from implementation to intent.
    IVD is the framework that makes AI collaboration reliable by making
    intent executable and verifiable. The book makes this case and teaches
    the methodology.

    IMPORTANT: The book sells the THESIS, not the framework checklist.
    IVD is one chapter — the methodology. The book's value is the worldview:
    "AI replaces decisions, not labor. The bottleneck is now intent."
    Blog posts that resonate never mention IVD by name. The thinking sells.

  approach: |
    - Blog posts on leocelis.com are first-draft chapters (written in public)
    - The content backlog maps to book chapters
    - Publish weekly → compile → edit → publish the book
    - No SaaS or tooling required — the book IS the product
    - IVD framework gets one chapter, not the whole book

  honesty_note: |
    The IVD success metrics in this intent (85-95% knowledge capture, 40%
    faster onboarding, etc.) are projections from Leo's own projects —
    NOT independently validated benchmarks. For the book: present as
    "in my experience" backed by real production examples, not as proven
    statistics. Readers trust experience over sourceless numbers.

  status: "Outlining — blog posts are accumulating as chapter drafts"
  date: "2026-03-03"

changelog:
  - version: 2.1
    date: "2026-02-09"
    change: "Added empirical refinement as canonical extension under Principle 3"
    reason: |
      Gap identified: when implementation reveals that the intent's assumptions are wrong
      (API returns different format, model accuracy differs from benchmarks, latency exceeds
      documented limits), no protocol existed for updating the intent with empirical evidence
      before continuing. This is distinct from code drift (P3 already covers code → intent sync)
      and from the stress test (P6 catches gaps before implementation). Empirical refinement
      catches gaps that only running the code reveals. Added:
      (1) Empirical Refinement Protocol (5-step: stop, record, update, enrich, continue) as
          P3 canonical extension in ivd_system_intent.yaml
      (2) framework.md new section under Principle 3
      (3) Agent Rule 5 in recipes/agent-rules-ivd.yaml
      (4) Book intents: Ch3 §3.3, Ch2 §2.3, book_system_intent Ch3 key_points
      (5) DECISIONS.md FDR-009

  - version: 2.0
    date: "2026-02-09"
    change: "Added cognitive_foundation to principles section — parametric vs. contextual knowledge systems"
    reason: |
      Research finding: LLMs allocate ~70% reliance on contextual knowledge and ~30% on parametric
      (training weights). Prose underloads the contextual channel, triggering parametric gap-filling
      = hallucination. Structured intent artifacts saturate the contextual channel with near-zero-entropy
      information. The lost-in-the-middle effect explains why verification protocol Step 1 (re-read
      from disk) is cognitively necessary, not optional. This is the theoretical foundation for why
      IVD works — previously unexplained at this level. Sources: 9-LLM study (2024), Huang et al.
      ICLR 2024, lost-in-the-middle (GPT-4/Claude 3, 2024), proactive interference (2025).
      Applied across: framework.md, book Ch1 §1.2, Ch2 §2.6, Ch3 §3.2+3.4, Ch12 §12.1.
      Implementation.version also updated to 2.0.

      implementation reveals flaws cheaper than implementation does. Root cause: the P6

      whether the intent itself is complete. Added:

      (3) New sub-principle: "AI stress-tests intent before implementing"
      (4) Three stress-test dimensions: constraint completeness, implementation anticipation,
          assumption challenge
      (5) framework.m

    change: "Added experimental authorship and evaluation optional sections"
    reason: |
      Two gaps identified in IVD's coverage of autonomous agent workflows:
      (1) Authorship: IVD assumed human-originated intent but AI agents increasingly
          originate intent (designing workflows, proposing structures). Authorship
          formalizes who can create/modify intent and what oversight is required.
      (2) Evaluation: IVD had binary verification (pass/fail constraints) but no
          formalized improvement loop. Evaluation extends verification to continuous
          quality improvement: evaluate → adjust → re-verify, with bounded iterations
          and escalation. Together they enable safe autonomy for AI agents.
  - version: 1.5
    date: "2026-02-09"
    change: "Added experimental roles optional section for AI agents with context-dependent behavior"
    reason: |
      AI agents that switch behavioral modes (implementer, reviewer, architect, teacher)
      had no IVD-canonical way to declare those profiles in intent. Without roles in intent,
      behavioral differences live only in code or prompts — violating P1 (Intent is Primary).
      Roles complement interface (WHAT vs HOW): interface defines tool capabilities; roles
      define how the agent uses those capabilities in different contexts. Status: Experimental
      — pending production validation in 2+ agents before promotion to Canonical.
    evidence: "Principle-by-principle gap analysis; real-world pattern: Cursor IDE modes (agent, plan, debug, ask)"
